childNodes.textContent:
Kitsoki mesa-sun favicon, inlined as a data URI: the SPA is built as a
         single go:embed'd file (vite-plugin-singlefile), so an external
         /favicon.svg reference would not be served. Source of truth:
         docs/branding/assets/mesa-sun-simple.svg (the small-size glyph).

Kitsoki "Mesa Sun" mark (docs/branding/logo.md, mesa-sun-simple.svg).
         Inlined as an SVG data URI so it survives vite-plugin-singlefile's
         single-file bundle and ships inside index.html — the Go web server
         (internal/runstatus/server) only routes "/" and "/rpc/*", so an
         external /favicon.svg would 404.

attributes.title: Meta mode — edit or ask about this story / kitsoki

node.textContent: A product manager has an idea for the slidey deck renderer: export a printable per-scene speaker-notes handout straight from the deck spec — a `slidey deck.json --notes out.md` flag, alongside the video, PDF, and web outputs. This tour walks that idea into a published PRD on the real slidey repo, no LLM in the loop.
This is slidey-dev: kitsoki's dev-story hub pointed at the real slidey checkout. PRDs land in the slidey tree at docs/prd/<slug>.md. We'll author the speaker-notes-export PRD here.
Click New session to start a fresh run of the hub against slidey. It opens in the chat, on the engineer's-day landing.
↳ Click the highlighted control to continue.

`prd` opens a discovery conversation, not a form. The PM pitches the feature in their own words — a per-scene notes handout exported from the deck spec — and the interviewer sharpens it into a crisp problem statement before any questions or drafting begin.

Discovery isn't one-shot. A structured clarification round asks the questions that most change the PRD — who's the actor (presenters rehearsing a talk), and the success metric (decks exported to notes without rendering a full video). The PM answers in the chat, and the accumulated Q&A becomes the PRD's grounding.

cycle 0 · draft attempt 0 · clarify rounds 1 · judge human

# PRD: Speaker-Notes Export

## Problem & Context
A slidey deck is one JSON spec that renders to video, PDF, and an
interactive web player — but a presenter rehearsing a talk has no
printable, per-scene handout of what each scene says.

## Target Users
Presenters and deck authors reviewing a deck before a talk.

## Goals
- A `slidey deck.json --notes out.md` flag that emits a per-scene
  handout (eyebrow + body + narration) from the same spec.
- Deterministic and LLM-free: same spec in, same notes out.

## Requirements
- Functional: one Markdown section per scene, in spec order.
- Non-functional: headline metric is
  decks-exported-to-notes-without-rendering-video.

## Open Questions
- Should scenes with no narration be skipped or emitted empty?

author the design from this PRD

A PRD isn't only prose. The PRD phase also produces a mockup of the feature — a printable per-scene speaker-notes handout — and kitsoki embeds it right in the run: a self-contained deck booted inline in the handoff room. The PM sees exactly what the feature looks like before a line of it is built, no separate renderer and no LLM.

Duplicate eyebrows → sections are still distinct (suffixed by index).
